output.answer:
## Technology Systems Being Replaced or Upgraded

The memos reference two distinct technology systems undergoing significant transitions:

---

### 1. ERP System (Tyler Technologies New World) — **Most Urgent**

The memos suggest the city's core enterprise resource planning system is approaching end-of-life. MAR-005 flags active problems: payroll calculation errors affecting FLSA overtime compliance and issues with fire shift trade processing. The vendor has not yet formally announced an end-of-life date, but the system is described as approaching it. Critically, Council budget authorization for a replacement procurement had not yet been confirmed as of that memo.

This is the most urgent of the two systems for two reasons:
- **Active errors are occurring now** — payroll miscalculations and FLSA compliance issues carry legal and financial risk that compounds over time.
- **The procurement window is closing** — if the city waits for the vendor to announce end-of-life before beginning procurement, it loses negotiating leverage and risks a forced, rushed transition. The curated analysis flags this as a medium-escalation risk with a clear next step: confirm Council authorization and begin procurement before the vendor acts.

---

### 2. Hexagon CAD/RMS System — On Track, Lower Urgency

The city is also implementing a new Hexagon computer-aided dispatch and records management system for public safety. As of MAR-010, six "blocker" tickets remained open, functional acceptance testing was underway, and the go-live target was late summer or early fall 2026. April milestones included RMS configuration, training curriculum development, and end-to-end testing.

Unlike the ERP, this project is described as **on track** — it is a planned replacement proceeding through a defined implementation schedule, not a system failing in production. The remaining uncertainty is whether the April milestones were met and whether the go-live date holds (MAR-010).

---

### 3. Parking Meter Software (IPS Group / Cloud Hosting) — Largely Resolved

A third, narrower technology item: the parking program transitioned its software to cloud hosting and engaged IPS Group for meter maintenance (Council approved a contract up to $200,000 in November 2025, per JAN-003). Battery and solar panel replacements were ongoing through early 2026 (JAN-003, FEB-018, APR-004). This is not a full system replacement and appears largely operational, though IPS meter battery issues caused some delays into March 2026 (APR-004).

---

### Summary

| System | Status | Urgency |
|---|---|---|
| ERP (Tyler New World) | Failing in production; procurement not yet authorized | 🔴 Most urgent |
| Hexagon CAD/RMS | Implementation on track; go-live late summer 2026 | 🟡 Monitor |
| IPS Parking Meters | Largely resolved; minor delays | 🟢 Low |

The ERP situation stands out because it combines **current operational harm** (payroll errors) with a **closing window for proactive action** before the vendor forces the city's hand — a combination the memos do not show for any other technology item.
